lesson.menu_title: What Is Happening Right Now -- Present Continuous for Live Situations and Travel Updates
lesson.menu_desc: Describe the airport, the delegation, the conference -- everything happening right now -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- What Is Happening Right Now -- Present Continuous for Travel Updates
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 4 -- Present Continuous for Travel Updates

listenings.text: Good afternoon, passengers. This is an important announcement for the delegation from Brazil. Your driver is waiting at Gate Two, near the main exit. The university team is preparing your welcome at this moment. Please remember: the conference is starting at four o'clock this afternoon, not in the morning. The rector is presenting the agenda tonight, at the dinner. If you need help, our staff is standing at the information desk, in the blue shirts. Thank you, and welcome to Seoul.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Grace, the conference coordinator. I am calling about tomorrow's schedule. Right now, we are finishing the final list of speakers. You are presenting at ten o'clock in the morning, in Room One. The other guests are arriving tonight, so we are having a small dinner at eight. Please bring your name badge. I am sending the full agenda to your email now. We are looking forward to your talk. See you soon, and have a good evening.

extra_audio.text: Good morning. I am arriving at the airport in Seoul now. Right now, the delegation is waiting in the lobby. This afternoon, we are meeting the rector. Tomorrow, I am presenting my work in English.

paras: It is Thursday morning, and Maria Clara is arriving at Incheon Airport, in Seoul. She is walking to the exit with her small suitcase. Outside, a driver is waiting for the delegation from Brazil. Right now, Maria Clara is not feeling nervous. She is smiling, because this is a big moment.

At the university, the team is preparing the conference room. Some people are putting names on the tables, and others are testing the microphones. The rector is reading the agenda one more time. In two hours, the guests are arriving, and the conference is starting.

Maria Clara is looking forward to this week. She is participating in an international forum, and tomorrow she is presenting her work in English, on her own. Her phone is ringing -- it is her granddaughter Clarinha. "Grandma is working in Seoul right now!" Maria Clara says, and they both laugh.
reading.source: A travel update written for this course

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Maria Clara is leaving Seoul and flying back home to Brazil.
Maria Clara is arriving in Seoul for a conference, and many things are happening right now.
Maria Clara is presenting her work at a university in Brazil today.

items: Maria Clara is arriving at Incheon Airport in Seoul.
The first paragraph says she is arriving at Incheon Airport, in Seoul.
The university team is preparing the conference room.
The second paragraph says the team is preparing the conference room.
Maria Clara is feeling very nervous at the airport.
The text says she is not feeling nervous; she is smiling.
Tomorrow, Maria Clara is presenting her work in English, on her own.
The third paragraph says tomorrow she is presenting her work in English, on her own.

items.situation: Someone asks: "What are you doing right now?"
tips: I am arriving at the airport in Seoul.
I am + verb-ing, for something happening now
Yes, the delegation is arriving this afternoon.
is + verb-ing for he / she / it / the group
I am looking forward to the conference.
I am looking forward to + noun or verb-ing
Yes, I am. I am presenting my work at ten o'clock.
The team is preparing the conference room.

items.situation: A speaker talks very fast and you miss a word.
tips: Sorry, I don't understand. Could you repeat, please?
Asking to repeat is control, not weakness.
